content.problemStatement:
Aditya is playing with NNN dominoes, which are arranged in a circle. For each iii (1‚Č§i<N1 \leq i \lt N1‚Č§i<N), domino i+1i+1i+1 follows domino iii; and domino 111 follows domino NNN.

More succinctly, the successor of domino iii is domino (i‚Äämod‚ÄäN)+1(i\bmod N) + 1(imodN)+1.

The iii-th domino has a weight of WiW_iWi‚Äč and an absorption factor of KiK_iKi‚Äč.

A domino will fall if and only if it is pushed with a force of at least WiW_iWi‚Äč.

When Aditya pushes domino iii with a force of FFF, the following occurs:


If F<WiF \lt W_iF<Wi‚Äč, nothing happens.
Otherwise, domino iii falls down, and will push its successor with a certain amount of force.

Specifically, domino (i‚Äämod‚ÄäN)+1(i\bmod N)+1(imodN)+1 will be pushed with a force of (Wi+max‚Ā°(0,F‚ąíKi))(W_i + \max(0, F - K_i))(Wi‚Äč+max(0,F‚ąíKi‚Äč)).


This transfer of pushing force will continue till either all the dominoes have fallen, or some domino doesn't receive enough force to fall.
Find the minimum force Aditya needs to apply on a **single domino** such that it will cause all NNN dominoes to fall.

That is, find the smallest integer FFF for which there exists a domino iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), such that pushing domino iii with a force of FFF will result in all NNN dominoes falling.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of dominoes.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers W1,W2,‚Ä¶,WNW_1, W_2, \ldots, W_NW1‚Äč,W2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,WN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the weights of the dominoes.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers K1,K2,‚Ä¶,KNK_1, K_2, \ldots, K_NK1‚Äč,K2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,KN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the absorption factors of the dominoes.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum force that needs to be applied to a single domino, so that all NNN dominoes fall.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3‚čÖ1053\cdot 10^53‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider applying a force of F=5F = 5F=5 on domino 444. The process is as follows:

5‚Č•W4=55\geq W_4 = 55‚Č•W4‚Äč=5, so domino 444 falls.

The force transferred to the next domino is W4+max‚Ā°(0,5‚ąíK4)=5+5=10W_4 + \max(0, 5-K_4) = 5 + 5 = 10W4‚Äč+max(0,5‚ąíK4‚Äč)=5+5=10.


10‚Č•W1=110\geq W_1 = 110‚Č•W1‚Äč=1, so domino 111 falls.

The force transferred is W1+max‚Ā°(0,10‚ąíK1)=1+9=10W_1 + \max(0, 10-K_1) = 1 + 9 = 10W1‚Äč+max(0,10‚ąíK1‚Äč)=1+9=10.


10‚Č•W2=210\geq W_2 = 210‚Č•W2‚Äč=2, so domino 222 falls.

The force transferred is W2+max‚Ā°(0,10‚ąíK2)=2+8=10W_2 + \max(0, 10-K_2) = 2+8 = 10W2‚Äč+max(0,10‚ąíK2‚Äč)=2+8=10.


10‚Č•W3=610\geq W_3 = 610‚Č•W3‚Äč=6, so domino 333 falls and we're done.

You may verify that applying a force of 444 or less isn't enough to make everything fall, no matter where Aditya starts.
Test case 222: Consider applying a force of F=4F = 4F=4 on domino 222. The process is as follows:

4‚Č•W2=24\geq W_2 = 24‚Č•W2‚Äč=2, so domino 222 falls.

The force transferred to the next domino is W2+max‚Ā°(0,4‚ąíK2)=2+3=5W_2 + \max(0, 4-K_2) = 2 + 3 = 5W2‚Äč+max(0,4‚ąíK2‚Äč)=2+3=5.


5‚Č•W3=55\geq W_3 = 55‚Č•W3‚Äč=5, so domino 333 falls.

The force transferred is W3+max‚Ā°(0,5‚ąíK3)=5+0=5W_3 + \max(0, 5-K_3) = 5 + 0 = 5W3‚Äč+max(0,5‚ąíK3‚Äč)=5+0=5.


5‚Č•W4=25\geq W_4 = 25‚Č•W4‚Äč=2, so domino 444 falls.

The force transferred is W4+max‚Ā°(0,5‚ąíK4)=2+1=3W_4 + \max(0, 5-K_4) = 2+1 = 3W4‚Äč+max(0,5‚ąíK4‚Äč)=2+1=3.


3‚Č•W1=33\geq W_1 = 33‚Č•W1‚Äč=3, so domino 111 falls and we're done.

Similarly here also it can be verified that applying a force of 333 or less isn't enough to make everything fall, no matter where Aditya starts.
